title: HVALS
description: Returns all values in a hash.
complexity: O(N) where N is the size of the hash.

returns.description: All the fields values contained into a hash.

params.description: streaming channel that receives a call for every value.
params.name: channel

params.description: streaming channel that receives a call for every value.
params.name: channel

returns.description: Long count of the keys. @deprecated since 6.0 in favor of consuming large results through the org.reactivestreams.Publisher returned by #hvals.

params.description: streaming channel that receives a call for every value.
params.name: channel

returns.description: Long count of the keys. @deprecated since 6.0 in favor of consuming large results through the org.reactivestreams.Publisher returned by #hvals.

params.description: The flags to use for this operation.
params.name: flags

returns.description: List of values in the hash, or an empty list when key does not exist.

content:
Returns all values in the hash stored at `key`. ## Examples {{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hvals" description="Foundational: Retrieve all values from a hash using HVALS (returns only values without field names, useful when you only need the data)" difficulty="beginner" >}} redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello" (integer) 1 redis> HSET myhash field2 "World" (integer) 1 redis> HVALS myhash 1) "Hello" 2) "World" {{< /clients-example >}} Give these commands a try in the interactive console: {{% redis-cli %}} HSET myhash field1 "Hello" HSET myhash field2 "World" HVALS myhash {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="hvals-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values in the hash, or an empty list when the key does not exist -tab-sep- [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of values in the hash, or an empty list when the key does not exist. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
(integer) 1
redis> HSET myhash field2 "World"
(integer) 1
redis> HVALS myhash
1) "Hello"
2) "World"
intro: Returns all values in the hash stored at `key`.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Returns all values in the hash stored at `key`.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{< clients-example set="cmds_hash" step="hvals" description="Foundational: Retrieve all values from a hash using HVALS (returns only values without field names, useful when you only need the data)" difficulty="beginner" >}}
redis> HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
(integer) 1
redis> HSET myhash field2 "World"
(integer) 1
redis> HVALS myhash
1) "Hello"
2) "World"
{{< /clients-example >}}

Give these commands a try in the interactive console:

{{% redis-cli %}}
HSET myhash field1 "Hello"
HSET myhash field2 "World"
HVALS myhash
{{% /redis-cli %}}

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility
sections.content:
| Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | |
